brands.name: AmericInn by Wyndham
brands.notes: Midscale hotel brand franchised by Wyndham Hotels & Resorts (acquired from Northcott Hospitality in 2017), with 230 hotels in 20 states as of 12/31/2025 and a stated push to grow 'from a Midwest player to a national name.' This is an institutional-scale hotel investment, not a small-business franchise. Wyndham does not publish a state-by-state availability list - development is negotiated market by market via development.wyndhamhotels.com - so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Heavy geographic concentration: 230 hotels in only 20 states
riskFlags.detail: The brand remains concentrated in Midwest suburban and interstate markets (230 hotels across 20 states as of year-end 2025), so brand awareness - and the reservation-system lift a franchisee is paying for - is unproven in most of the country Wyndham is now targeting for expansion.
riskFlags.source: Wyndham Hotels & Resorts development site - AmericInn by Wyndham

riskFlags.title: Land excluded from headline investment figures
riskFlags.detail: FDD-derived estimates put a typical 3-story, 75-room new build at $5,996,272-$8,576,730 excluding land acquisition costs; Vetted Biz's 2024 FDD-derived range runs $7.7M-$11M. Conversions of existing hotels can enter far lower (from ~$272,564), making the true all-in cost highly project-dependent.
riskFlags.source: The FDD Exchange (AmericInn FDD summary); Vetted Biz FDD analysis

riskFlags.title: Ownership change: brand acquired by Wyndham in 2017
riskFlags.detail: Wyndham Hotel Group completed its acquisition of AmericInn and its hotel management company from Northcott Hospitality in October 2017 (~200 hotels at the time), folding the brand into the world's largest hotel franchisor's 20+ brand portfolio, where it competes for development resources against sister midscale brands like Baymont and Wingate.
riskFlags.source: PR Newswire - Wyndham Hotel Group Completes Acquisition of AmericInn

liquidCapitalMin.note: Minimum cash per Vetted Biz FDD analysis; actual requirement scales with the hotel project.

totalInvestmentMin.source: The FDD Exchange / Franchise Direct AmericInn FDD summary
totalInvestmentMin.note: Low end is a 100-room conversion of an existing hotel ($272,564-$3,705,733). New construction of a typical 3-story, 75-room AmericInn runs $5,996,272-$8,576,730 (excl. land); Vetted Biz's 2024 figures run $7,738,476-$10,960,034.

totalInvestmentMax.note: New-construction high end; excludes land acquisition.

franchiseFee.source: The FDD Exchange / Vetted Biz AmericInn FDD summaries
franchiseFee.note: Initial fee commonly quoted at $35,000 (hotel application fees typically scale with room count).

royaltyPercent.source: Vetted Biz - AmericInn Franchise Insights; Franchise Direct

adFundPercent.source: Vetted Biz - AmericInn Franchise Insights; Franchise Direct
adFundPercent.note: 2% marketing/program fee on gross room revenue.

totalUnits.source: Wyndham Hotels & Resorts development site - AmericInn by Wyndham
totalUnits.note: 230 hotels in 20 states as of 12/31/2025; ScrapeHero independently counts 230 US locations as of March 2026.

yearFounded.source: Wyndham Hotels & Resorts development site (brand inception)
yearFounded.note: Brand inception 1984; acquired by Wyndham in 2017.

trainingWeeks.note: Two weeks at corporate headquarters (Parsippany, NJ).

isGrowing.source: Hotel Management (HM Exclusive); Wyndham development site
isGrowing.note: Opened more hotels in H1 2024 than in any year since the 2017 acquisition (228 hotels mid-2024, 230 by end of 2025) - modest net growth on a small base.

fddAvailable.source: The FDD Exchange (AmericInn FDD on file)

brands.name: Rita's Italian Ice & Frozen Custard
brands.notes: Seasonal Italian-ice and frozen-custard chain (founded outside Philadelphia) with ~569 US shops, under new private-equity majority ownership (Maple Park Capital Partners, Jan 2025). Item 19 results are highly dispersed and the business is seasonal in most markets. The franchisor does not publish a public state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Wide Item 19 performance dispersion: bottom-tier shops average $212K
riskFlags.detail: 2024 Item 19 splits 520 reporting shops into thirds: top tier averaged $544,799 gross sales, middle tier $338,628, bottom tier just $212,106 (range floor $14,620) - on an investment that can exceed $770K, low-tier results imply very long or no payback.
riskFlags.source: Rita's 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter review)

riskFlags.title: Franchisee Chapter 11 bankruptcy filing (June 2025)
riskFlags.detail: Greene Family Enterprises LLC, a St. Johns, FL Rita's franchisee, filed for Chapter 11 protection in June 2025 listing $500K-$1M in liabilities against $100K-$500K in assets - a recent, concrete example of unit-level distress.
riskFlags.source: TheStreet

riskFlags.title: Private-equity majority stake acquired January 2025
riskFlags.detail: Maple Park Capital Partners acquired a majority stake in Rita's in January 2025. New ownership is investing in growth infrastructure, but PE ownership changes can shift fee enforcement, vendor programs, and development priorities mid-agreement.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Chatter 2025 FDD review (news section)

riskFlags.title: Longstanding franchisee complaints documented
riskFlags.detail: Franchisee-complaint forums document recurring allegations from former Rita's operators about training quality, unreliable ROI expectations, and franchisor responsiveness; the franchisor has also litigated against franchisees (e.g., 2013 E.D. Pa. suit over unauthorized products).
riskFlags.source: Unhappy Franchisee - Rita's Italian Ice Franchise Complaints; Pennsylvania Record

totalInvestmentMin.note: Standard shop Item 7 range $315,233-$770,542; directory sources citing from $22,000 reflect non-standard/satellite formats.

franchiseFee.note: FDD discloses $10,000-$35,000 depending on format; $35,000 is the standard shop fee.

royaltyPercent.note: 6.5% of estimated sales (mix items) or gross sales (non-mix items).

adFundPercent.note: 3% advertising contribution plus a 2% minimum local advertising requirement (3% ceiling).

avgUnitRevenue.source: Rita's 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter review) - derived
avgUnitRevenue.note: Weighted average derived from the FDD's tiered 2024 disclosure (top/middle/bottom thirds of 520 shops averaged $544,799 / $338,628 / $212,106). The FDD presents tiers, not a single systemwide AUV.

totalUnits.source: Rita's 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter review)

yearFounded.source: Vetted Biz - Rita's Italian Ice franchise analysis
yearFounded.note: Founded in Bensalem, PA by Bob Tumolo.

yearFranchising.source: Vetted Biz - Rita's Italian Ice franchise analysis

isGrowing.source: Rita's 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter review)
isGrowing.note: Franchised units -9 (2022), +18 (2023), +18 (2024); company also began operating a handful of corporate shops (5 by end of 2024). Texas expansion ongoing.

seasonalityNotes.value: Highly seasonal: most shops operate a spring-summer 'season' (the FDD's Item 19 measures the 2024 operating season), with many East Coast locations closed in winter.
seasonalityNotes.source: Rita's 2025 FDD Item 19 framing (via Franchise Chatter review)

brands.name: Pro Image Sports
brands.notes: The largest franchised licensed-sports-apparel retailer in the US (~150 stores, all franchised, HQ Centerville, UT), selling pro and collegiate team merchandise primarily from mall and outlet locations. Ranked #168 on Entrepreneur's 2025 Franchise 500. The franchisor does not publish a state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Inventory-heavy, license-dependent retail model
riskFlags.detail: Stores sell licensed professional and collegiate apparel and headwear; franchisee economics depend on league/vendor licensing programs and merchandise costs the franchisee does not control, and the Item 7 range spans $109,750-$605,500 largely because of inventory and build-out variability by venue.
riskFlags.source: Pro Image Sports franchisor site; Vetted Biz FDD analysis

riskFlags.title: Mall/foot-traffic venue dependence
riskFlags.detail: The chain's store base is concentrated in enclosed malls and similar retail venues, exposing franchisees to landlord leverage and secular mall-traffic decline - the same structural risk documented for other venue-dependent retail franchises.
riskFlags.source: Pro Image Sports franchise opportunity site (store format descriptions)

riskFlags.title: Clean FDD: no litigation or bankruptcy disclosed
riskFlags.detail: FDD-tracking services report Pro Image Sports does not disclose lawsuits or bankruptcy information in its FDD - a comparatively clean Item 3/Item 4 record.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - Pro Image Sports Franchise Insights

liquidCapitalMin.source: Vetted Biz - Pro Image Sports Franchise Insights
liquidCapitalMin.note: Minimum cash required per Vetted Biz FDD analysis.

totalInvestmentMin.source: Pro Image Sports 2024 FDD (via Vetted Biz; SHARPSHEETS cites $110,000-$615,000)

totalInvestmentMax.source: Pro Image Sports 2024 FDD (via Vetted Biz)

franchiseFee.source: Pro Image Sports 2024 FDD (via Vetted Biz and SHARPSHEETS)

royaltyPercent.source: Pro Image Sports 2024 FDD (via Vetted Biz and SHARPSHEETS)

avgUnitRevenue.source: Vetted Biz - Pro Image Sports Franchise Insights (FDD-derived)
avgUnitRevenue.note: Yearly gross sales per Vetted Biz analysis; estimated payback 5.6-7.6 years.

totalUnits.source: Vetted Biz (149-155 range across sources); SHARPSHEETS 2024 FDD data

yearFounded.source: Vetted Biz - Pro Image Sports Franchise Insights
yearFounded.note: Vetted Biz lists 1996 for both founding and franchising of the current franchisor entity (Pro Image Franchise L.C.); the original Pro Image brand dates to the mid-1980s.

yearFranchising.source: Vetted Biz - Pro Image Sports Franchise Insights

trainingWeeks.source: Vetted Biz - Pro Image Sports Franchise Insights

renewalTermYears.source: SHARPSHEETS / FDD Exchange Pro Image Sports 2024 FDD summaries
renewalTermYears.note: 10-year initial term; successor terms of up to 5 years available if in good standing.

mobileBased.source: Pro Image Sports franchisor site (retail storefront model)

fddAvailable.source: The FDD Exchange (Pro Image Sports 2024 FDD)

item19Available.source: Vetted Biz (publishes FDD-derived yearly gross sales)

brands.name: Biggby Coffee
brands.notes: Michigan-born drive-thru/cafe coffee franchise (B Cubed Inc., East Lansing) with 420 franchised stores and zero company-owned units, targeting 1,000 stores by 2028. Currently operating in roughly 13 states with named emerging markets (Boise, Memphis, Nashville, Atlanta, Charlotte, Winston-Salem, Raleigh, Chicago, Virginia Beach); no full state-by-state availability list is published, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Heavy regional concentration in Michigan and the Midwest
riskFlags.detail: The system operates in only about 13 states with its core in Michigan and adjacent Midwest markets; expansion states (ID, GA, VA, TN, NC, FL) are recent and thin, so brand pull outside the home region is unproven for new franchisees - and the FDD even carves out a reduced 5% royalty for certain legacy Michigan operators.
riskFlags.source: Biggby Coffee press (2022 year-end expansion release); Franchise Chatter 2025 FDD review

riskFlags.title: Consumer class action over non-dairy milk surcharges
riskFlags.detail: Bower v. Biggby Coffee is a class action over surcharges for non-dairy milk alternatives - part of a wave of similar suits against coffee chains. Brand-level consumer litigation can force systemwide pricing/menu changes that land on franchisees.
riskFlags.source: Keith Gibson Law, P.C. (class action counsel page)

riskFlags.title: Aggressive growth target: 1,000 stores by 2028
riskFlags.detail: Management targets 1,000 stores and $1B in system sales by 2028 - more than doubling the 420-store base in about three years. Hyper-growth pushes can strain site selection, training, and support quality for incoming franchisees.
riskFlags.source: Fast Casual - Biggby Coffee on track to have 1K locations by 2028

riskFlags.title: Non-drive-thru stores earn materially less
riskFlags.detail: 2024 Item 19: drive-thru stores averaged $747,227 gross sales with 13.1% EBITDA margin vs $582,042 and 8.8% for non-drive-thru stores - format choice materially changes the return profile, and the bottom store reported just $262,729.
riskFlags.source: Biggby Coffee 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter review)

totalInvestmentMin.source: Biggby Coffee 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)
totalInvestmentMin.note: Whitebox leasehold format low end; modular locations run $533,500-$949,000 and site-built drive-thru up to $1,011,500.

totalInvestmentMax.source: Biggby Coffee 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)

franchiseFee.source: Biggby Coffee 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)

royaltyPercent.source: Biggby Coffee 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)
royaltyPercent.note: 6% of gross sales (5% for certain legacy Michigan operators).

adFundPercent.source: Biggby Coffee 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)
adFundPercent.note: Brand fund: greater of $100/week or 3% of gross sales, plus minimum local advertising of up to 3%.

avgUnitRevenue.source: Biggby Coffee 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter review)
avgUnitRevenue.note: 2024 average gross sales across all 358 reporting stores; median $702,752; average EBITDA $91,984 (12.5%) across 253 profitability-reporting units.

totalUnits.source: Biggby Coffee 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter review)
totalUnits.note: All franchised; 0 company-owned. Franchisor press cites 430 stores in 13 states with 120 signed agreements for future locations.

yearFounded.source: Biggby Coffee corporate site (brand history; 2025 brand refresh billed as first in 30 years)
yearFounded.note: First store opened in East Lansing, MI in March 1995.

isGrowing.source: Biggby Coffee 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter review)

multiUnitAvailable.note: Over half of Biggby franchisees own more than one location; Area Representative program active in 7 states.

mobileBased.source: Biggby Coffee franchisor site (cafe/drive-thru building model)

fddAvailable.source: The FDD Exchange (Biggby Coffee 2025 FDD); Franchise Chatter review

brands.name: X-Golf
brands.notes: Indoor golf-simulator lounge franchise (X-Golf America, ~126-138 US locations) combining simulator bays with a bar/restaurant component. Capital-intensive build (~$1M-$1.9M) against modest FDD-derived average revenue, and the franchisor is currently embroiled in insider/franchise-act litigation in Michigan. No published state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Active Michigan litigation including franchise act violation allegations
riskFlags.detail: X-Golf Michigan, LLC sued X-Golf Franchise Corporation and an executive in January 2025 for tortious interference with its Michigan franchisee relationships; an April 2026 Oakland County Business Court opinion in the case references alleged violations of the Michigan Franchise Investment Law. Litigation between the franchisor and its largest regional developer is a material governance red flag.
riskFlags.source: Michigan Courts - Oakland County Business Court opinion, X-Golf Michigan, LLC (C06-2025-212754-CB)

riskFlags.title: Shareholder derivative suit alleged revenue siphoning by insiders
riskFlags.detail: A California shareholder derivative action alleged certain X-Golf directors/officers siphoned revenues to X-Golf Michigan, LLC, an entity they controlled, by usurping corporate opportunities; it was voluntarily dismissed in February 2025 with claims expected to re-surface as counterclaims in the ongoing Michigan suit.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz / FranPass litigation summaries of X-Golf FDD Item 3

riskFlags.title: FDD-derived revenue implies a very long payback
riskFlags.detail: Vetted Biz's FDD-derived average yearly gross sales of $582,000 against a $1.13M-$1.76M investment yields an estimated payback period of 18.9-20.9 years; another FDD-derived source (SHARPSHEETS) cites average unit revenue as low as $413,821. Verify current Item 19 cohorts carefully before investing.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - X Golf Franchise Insights

liquidCapitalMin.source: Vetted Biz - X Golf Franchise Insights

totalInvestmentMin.note: SHARPSHEETS' FDD-derived range is $994,000-$1,940,000; figures vary by FDD year and venue size.

franchiseFee.source: X-Golf FDD (via Vetted Biz and SHARPSHEETS)

royaltyPercent.source: X-Golf FDD (via Vetted Biz and SHARPSHEETS)

avgUnitRevenue.source: Vetted Biz - X Golf Franchise Insights (FDD-derived)
avgUnitRevenue.note: Average yearly gross sales per Vetted Biz; SHARPSHEETS cites $413,821. Both imply long payback vs the $1M+ build cost.

totalUnits.source: Vetted Biz - X Golf Franchise Insights

yearFounded.source: Vetted Biz - X Golf Franchise Insights
yearFounded.note: US franchisor (X-Golf America); the simulator technology originated in South Korea in the 2000s.

yearFranchising.source: Vetted Biz - X Golf Franchise Insights

trainingWeeks.source: Vetted Biz - X Golf Franchise Insights

fddAvailable.source: The FDD Exchange (X-Golf FDD on file); Vetted Biz

brands.name: Re-Bath
brands.notes: America's largest complete bathroom remodeling franchise (HQ Tempe, AZ; founded 1979 as a bathtub-liner business), with close to 100 franchisees operating across 43 states. Converted from a dealer network to a franchise model starting in 2010. Franchisor markets new and resale territories; no public state-by-state availability list, so the nationwide convention below reflects the franchisor's own territory marketing.

riskFlags.title: Franchisor litigation against terminated franchisee (Foothills Service Solutions)
riskFlags.detail: In ReBath LLC v. Foothills Service Solutions Co. (D. Ariz. 2021), Re-Bath obtained injunctive relief against a defaulted/terminated franchisee that kept operating - evidence both of franchisee failure within the system and of an enforcement-minded franchisor.
riskFlags.source: Lewitt Hackman franchise law alert

riskFlags.title: Business model converted from dealer network to franchise in 2010
riskFlags.detail: Re-Bath operated for ~30 years as a bathtub-liner dealer network before converting to a franchise model in 2010 and expanding into full bathroom remodels and accessibility work - the franchise system as currently structured is younger than the 1979 founding date suggests.
riskFlags.source: Re-Bath corporate site - Our Story; Franchising.com Re-Bath business overview

riskFlags.title: Minimum royalties apply regardless of sales
riskFlags.detail: Royalty is 5-6% of installed sales with a minimum of $575 per month in year one (minimums escalate thereafter per FDD summaries) - a fixed obligation while a new territory ramps, on top of a 2% advertising contribution.
riskFlags.source: SHARPSHEETS Re-Bath FDD analysis; Franchise Payback

nationwide.note: Franchisor markets new and resale territories across the US (franchisees currently in 43 states). Confirm specific territory availability with the franchisor.

totalInvestmentMin.note: Includes showroom/warehouse/office, vehicles, equipment, and 3 months of additional funds.

royaltyPercent.source: Re-Bath FDD (via SHARPSHEETS and Franchise Payback)
royaltyPercent.note: 5-6% of installed sales, with a $575/month minimum in year one.

adFundPercent.note: 2% of the previous month's gross revenues.

avgUnitRevenue.note: Yearly gross sales per Vetted Biz analysis; 2021 FDD data showed a 47.1% average gross profit margin for 500K-1.25M population territories.

totalUnits.source: Vetted Biz / Franchising.com (150+ locations, ~100 franchisees in 43 states)

yearFranchising.source: Re-Bath corporate site - Our Story (dealer-to-franchise conversion)
yearFranchising.note: Predecessor dealer network dates to 1979; franchise model adopted beginning 2010.

fddAvailable.source: Franchise Direct / SHARPSHEETS Re-Bath FDD summaries

brands.name: Petland
brands.notes: One of the only remaining franchises selling puppies and kittens at retail (founded 1967, Chillicothe, OH; franchising since 1972), with ~92 US stores (67 franchised + 25 company-owned) plus international licensees. CRITICAL diligence area: the live-animal retail model faces CDC-documented zoonotic outbreaks, sustained animal-welfare litigation/campaigns, and a growing list of states and 340+ cities that ban retail dog/cat sales outright - shrinking the brand's legally addressable map. No published state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: CDC-linked multistate Campylobacter outbreaks tied to Petland puppies
riskFlags.detail: CDC and state health departments linked multistate outbreaks of multi-drug-resistant Campylobacter infections to contact with pet-store puppies, with 100+ cases tied to Petland stores across investigations (13+ states, multiple hospitalizations), spawning personal-injury suits including hospitalized customers in Michigan and Ohio.
riskFlags.source: AboutLawsuits.com (CDC outbreak coverage); Fox 47 News (Petland Novi lawsuit)

riskFlags.title: Retail pet-sale bans are closing markets to the core business model
riskFlags.detail: Multiple states (including California, New York, Illinois and Maryland) and 340+ US cities have banned or restricted retail sales of dogs and cats, directly outlawing Petland's signature live-animal model in those jurisdictions and forcing store-level model changes or exits.
riskFlags.source: Animal Defense Coalition - Petland; municipal ordinance tracking (Naperville, IL cited as 341st city)

riskFlags.title: Sustained animal-welfare investigations and deceptive-practices lawsuits
riskFlags.detail: Humane World for Animals (formerly HSUS) has run a years-long investigation campaign alleging Petland stores source from puppy mills, and consumer lawsuits allege deceptive sales and predatory financing (e.g., a financed puppy whose total cost would reach $26,000 with interest). Reputational pressure is persistent and organized, including in-store protests.
riskFlags.source: Humane World for Animals - Exposing Petland's Puppy Mill Practices

riskFlags.title: High company-owned share for a franchise system
riskFlags.detail: 25 of 92 US stores (~27%) are company-owned per 2024 FDD-derived data - the franchisor competes operationally alongside its franchisees and has direct exposure to the same regulatory headwinds.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Payback - Petland FDD data

totalInvestmentMin.note: Franchise Direct's summary cites $280,000-$1,034,000; range depends heavily on store size and live-animal habitat build-out.

franchiseFee.note: Up to $40,000; some 2025 FDD-derived sources cite $50,000.

royaltyPercent.note: 4.5% of gross revenues for the first two stores; drops to 2.25% for third and subsequent stores after 3 years' compliant multi-store operation.

adFundPercent.note: Up to 0.5% national ad fund; franchisees must additionally spend 5% of gross revenues on local advertising.

avgUnitRevenue.note: Yearly gross sales per Vetted Biz analysis.

totalUnits.source: Petland 2025 FDD outlet data (via Franchise Payback)
totalUnits.note: US only: 67 franchised + 25 company-owned (2024). International licensees (e.g., Japan, Canada, Brazil) push brand-wide counts higher (~320 per some directories).

yearFounded.note: Founded by Ed Kunzelman in Chillicothe, OH.

yearFranchising.source: Petland corporate site (first franchised store, Portsmouth, OH)

multiUnitAvailable.source: Petland FDD royalty structure (reduced 2.25% royalty for 3rd+ stores, via Franchise Direct)

fddAvailable.source: 2025 Petland FDD on file with Minnesota Department of Commerce (public registration)

brands.name: Tint World
brands.notes: Automotive styling franchise (window tint, paint protection film, wraps, audio/electronics, ceramic coatings; HQ Boca Raton, FL) with 151 locations across 35 US states plus Canada and the Middle East and a stated goal of 300 locations by end of 2026. Founded 1982, franchising since 2007. Franchisor markets territories across the US and internationally.

riskFlags.title: Development pipeline (82 stores) is more than half the open base (151)
riskFlags.detail: The franchisor touts 82 locations in development against 151 open and a 300-store goal by end of 2026 - an aggressive doubling that depends on new franchisees opening on schedule; buyers should pressure-test support capacity and whether advertised momentum reflects signed agreements rather than open stores.
riskFlags.source: International Franchise Association - Tint World expansion release

riskFlags.title: Marketed revenue claims exceed FDD-derived averages
riskFlags.detail: FDD-derived analysis puts average unit revenue around $743,270, while some directory/lead-gen sources promote a ~$1.35M median AUV and 15-25% margins - verify the actual Item 19 cohort definitions in the current FDD before relying on marketed figures.
riskFlags.source: SHARPSHEETS Tint World FDD analysis (vs. franchiseinvestordata marketing figures)

riskFlags.title: Transfer fee equals 50% of the initial franchise fee
riskFlags.detail: FDD summaries disclose a transfer fee of 50% of the initial franchise fee (~$25,000) plus a $250/month technology fee and $500-$1,000/month ad-fund minimums on top of the 6% royalty and 2% marketing contribution - exit and fixed costs worth modeling.
riskFlags.source: PeerSense Tint World FDD summary

nationwide.source: Tint World franchise site (franchisor); IFA expansion release
nationwide.note: Franchisor markets territories across the US (presence in 35 states) and internationally (Canada, Saudi Arabia, UAE). Confirm specific territory availability with the franchisor.

franchiseFee.source: Tint World 2025 FDD (via SHARPSHEETS and PeerSense)

adFundPercent.note: 2% of sales plus $500-$1,000/month ad-fund minimums and a $250/month technology fee.

avgUnitRevenue.source: Tint World FDD performance data (via SHARPSHEETS analysis)
avgUnitRevenue.note: Some directory sources promote a ~$1.35M median AUV; rely on the current FDD Item 19.

totalUnits.source: International Franchise Association - Tint World expansion release
totalUnits.note: 151 locations across 35 US states and 4 countries, with 82 more in development.

yearFounded.source: Tint World corporate site - Our History
yearFounded.note: Grew to six company locations in South Florida before franchising.

yearFranchising.source: Tint World corporate site - Our History

isGrowing.source: International Franchise Association - Tint World expansion release
isGrowing.note: 151 open with 82 in development; goal of 300 locations by end of 2026.

internationalAvailability.source: Tint World franchise site (Canada, Saudi Arabia, UAE; worldwide opportunities)

transferFee.source: PeerSense Tint World FDD summary (50% of initial franchise fee)

businessModelType.source: Tint World franchisor site (retail automotive styling centers)

mobileBased.source: Tint World franchisor site (fixed styling-center model; some mobile services offered from centers)

fddAvailable.source: SHARPSHEETS / PeerSense Tint World 2025 FDD summaries

brands.name: Stanley Steemer
brands.notes: Iconic carpet/upholstery cleaning, air-duct, and water-restoration franchise (family-owned since 1947, Columbus/Dublin, OH; franchising since 1972) with 267 US outlets - 210 franchised and 57 company-owned - covering 49 states. The system is mature and essentially built out: franchised unit count has drifted slightly down for years, so most entry is via resale of existing territories. No published open-territory list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Slow franchised-unit attrition in a mature system
riskFlags.detail: Franchised outlets declined from 217 to 210 across 2022-2024 (-7, about -3.2%) while company-owned units held at 57 - the system is not expanding, and new buyers are mostly acquiring existing territories rather than opening fresh ones.
riskFlags.source: Stanley Steemer 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter review)

riskFlags.title: Extreme territory-driven performance dispersion
riskFlags.detail: 2024 Item 19: nationwide average gross sales were $1,744,505 across 208 reporting units, but the median was just $1,196,358 and the range ran $33,616-$13,445,081; regional averages span $739,678 (Great Plains) to $3,520,891 (Southeast). Only 65 of 208 units met or exceeded the average.
riskFlags.source: Stanley Steemer 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter review)

riskFlags.title: Population-priced fee and heavy advertising obligations
riskFlags.detail: The initial franchise fee is ~$20,000 per 100,000 territory population ($20,000-$100,000 typical), and ongoing obligations include a 7% royalty on core-business sales plus advertising obligations of up to 10% of gross sales (including a national ad fee of up to 4%) - consistent with the brand's famously heavy media spend, but a large fixed burden.
riskFlags.source: Stanley Steemer 2025 FDD fee tables (via Franchise Chatter review)

totalInvestmentMin.source: Stanley Steemer 2025 FDD Item 7 (via Franchise Chatter review)

totalInvestmentMax.source: Stanley Steemer 2025 FDD Item 7 (via Franchise Chatter review)

franchiseFee.source: Stanley Steemer 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)
franchiseFee.note: Approximately $20,000 per 100,000 population in the territory; typical total $20,000-$100,000.

royaltyPercent.source: Stanley Steemer 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)
royaltyPercent.note: 7% of core-business sales; 3% on related-business sales; negotiated annual minimums apply.

adFundPercent.source: Stanley Steemer 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)
adFundPercent.note: National advertising fee up to 4% of monthly core/related sales; total advertising obligations can reach 10% of gross sales.

avgUnitRevenue.source: Stanley Steemer 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter review)
avgUnitRevenue.note: 2024 average gross sales across 208 reporting units; median $1,196,358; heavy regional dispersion.

totalUnits.source: Stanley Steemer 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter review)
totalUnits.note: 210 franchised + 57 company-owned at end of 2024; brand claims 280+ locations in 49 states.

yearFounded.source: Stanley Steemer corporate history (Jack Bates Carpet Cleaning Company, Columbus, OH)

yearFranchising.source: Stanley Steemer corporate history (incorporated Stanley Steemer International and began franchising 1972)

isGrowing.source: Stanley Steemer 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter review)

businessModelType.source: Stanley Steemer service lines (residential and commercial cleaning/restoration)

mobileBased.source: Stanley Steemer 2025 FDD Item 7 (truck-based service model; via Franchise Chatter review)
mobileBased.note: Truck-mounted service business operating from a warehouse/office, not a retail storefront.

brands.name: HomeWell Care Services
brands.notes: Non-medical in-home senior care franchise (founded 1996 as Hoffman's Home Helpers in Seattle; rebranded HomeWell Care Services after a 2018 ownership change; HQ Burkburnett/Fort Worth, TX) with 179 franchised territories operated by 93 businesses, zero company-owned, and strong recent unit growth. Sold to PE firm Main Post Partners with existing management in January 2026. Notable zero-initial-fee entry option in exchange for a doubled (10%) royalty. No published state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Right-skewed Item 19: roughly 7 in 10 territories fall below the published averages
riskFlags.detail: 2024 Item 19: single-territory businesses open 1+ years averaged $1,312,104 gross revenues but the median was $860,612 (range $94,472-$8,644,318) with only 32% meeting the average; multi-territory businesses averaged $3,200,790 (median $2,202,966) with only 30% meeting the average. Revenue figures say nothing about profit.
riskFlags.source: HomeWell Care Services 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter review)

riskFlags.title: Two ownership changes since 2018, now private-equity controlled
riskFlags.detail: Founder Joshua Hoffman sold HomeWell in 2018 to former Extended Stay America CEO Bruce Haase (who rebranded the system); in January 2026 the company was sold again to Main Post Partners alongside existing management. Successive ownership changes can shift fee enforcement and support priorities mid-agreement.
riskFlags.source: HomeWell corporate site - Our Story; franchise trade press on the Main Post Partners transaction

riskFlags.title: Zero-fee entry option carries a doubled 10% royalty
riskFlags.detail: HomeWell offers a $0 initial franchise fee option ($5,000 training fee at signing) in exchange for a 10% ongoing royalty versus 5% under the traditional $49,500-fee structure - cheaper to enter, materially more expensive at scale - and minimum royalty amounts apply based on territory count and tenure regardless of revenue.
riskFlags.source: HomeWell Care Services 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)

totalInvestmentMin.source: HomeWell Care Services 2025 FDD Item 7 (via Franchise Chatter review)
totalInvestmentMin.note: $54,401-$233,912; low end reflects the zero-initial-fee option and home-office start.

totalInvestmentMax.source: HomeWell Care Services 2025 FDD Item 7 (via Franchise Chatter review)

franchiseFee.source: HomeWell Care Services 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)
franchiseFee.note: Traditional option; a $0-fee option (with $5,000 training fee and 10% royalty) is also offered.

royaltyPercent.source: HomeWell Care Services 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)
royaltyPercent.note: 5% of monthly gross revenues under the traditional fee option (10% under the zero-fee option); minimums based on territory count and tenure.

adFundPercent.source: HomeWell Care Services 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)
adFundPercent.note: Brand development fund: greater of 2% or $1,000/month on the first $1M of revenues (1% above $1M), plus local marketing of the greater of 2% or $1,000/month.

avgUnitRevenue.source: HomeWell Care Services 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter review)
avgUnitRevenue.note: 2024 average gross revenues for 34 single-territory businesses open 1+ years; median $860,612. Multi-territory businesses averaged $3,200,790.

totalUnits.source: HomeWell Care Services 2025 FDD Item 20 (via Franchise Chatter review)
totalUnits.note: 179 franchised territories (93 operating businesses) at end of 2024; zero company-owned.

yearFounded.note: Founded by caregiver Joshua Hoffman in Seattle as Hoffman's Home Helpers; became HomeWell Senior Care in 2002.

yearFranchising.source: HomeWell corporate site - Our Story / franchise trade press
yearFranchising.note: Some directories cite 2003-2004 for the predecessor HomeWell Senior Care system; corporate history describes franchising expansion from 2013.

isGrowing.source: HomeWell Care Services 2025 FDD Item 20 (via Franchise Chatter review)

canRunFromHome.source: HomeWell 2025 FDD Item 7 (real estate line item $3,600-$9,000 for office space; via Franchise Chatter)
canRunFromHome.note: A small office is typically required for home-care licensure in most states.

mobileBased.source: HomeWell franchisor site (caregivers serve clients in their homes from a small admin office)

brands.name: American Poolplayers Association
brands.notes: The world's largest amateur pool league franchise (HQ Lake Saint Louis, MO; founded 1981, franchising since 1982): franchisees operate recreational 8-ball/9-ball leagues in bars and pool halls across a designated territory rather than a physical location. One of the lowest-cost franchises in the Franchise 500 (~$22K-$31K all-in), with leagues running in 13,000+ host venues across North America. No published open-territory list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: 20% royalty - among the highest rates in franchising
riskFlags.detail: Franchisees pay a royalty of 20% of the basic weekly fees charged to each league team (some directories describe it as 20% of monthly sales), plus advertising contributions of up to 5% depending on the approved market plan - an unusually high take relative to the small revenue base of a league operation.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Direct APA FDD summary; Vetted Biz

riskFlags.title: Revenue ceiling tied to territory population and venue saturation
riskFlags.detail: Income scales with the number of teams a territory's bars and pool halls can support; Vetted Biz flags local market saturation and the administrative load of running weekly league nights and tournaments as the model's core challenges - this is typically a part-time-to-modest-income business, not a high-AUV opportunity.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - American Poolplayers Association Franchise Insights

riskFlags.title: Litigation/bankruptcy disclosures present in FDD
riskFlags.detail: FDD-tracking services note that APA discloses lawsuits and/or bankruptcy information in its FDD Items 3/4; review the current document for specifics before signing.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - American Poolplayers Association Franchise Insights

liquidCapitalMin.note: Vetted Biz cites $5,000 minimum cash; Franchise Help cites $12,000 minimum liquid capital.

totalInvestmentMin.source: APA 2025 FDD Item 7 (via The FDD Exchange and Vetted Biz)

totalInvestmentMax.source: APA 2025 FDD Item 7 (via The FDD Exchange and Vetted Biz)

franchiseFee.note: Population-based: $10,000-$12,000 per Franchise Direct ($10,000-$14,000 per Vetted Biz).

royaltyPercent.note: 20% of the basic weekly fee charged to each team.

adFundPercent.note: Up to 5% of gross revenue depending on the approved marketing plan for the area.

totalUnits.note: US franchise territories; directories estimate ~360. Leagues operate in 13,000+ host venues across North America.

mobileBased.source: Franchise Direct APA FDD summary (territory-based league operation in third-party venues, no premises required)

fddAvailable.source: The FDD Exchange / Free FDD Library (APA 2025 FDD)

brands.name: A&W Restaurants
brands.notes: America's oldest restaurant franchise (root beer stand founded 1919 in Lodi, CA; franchising since the 1920s), known for root beer floats and burgers. Unusual governance: since the 2011 divestiture by Yum! Brands, the franchisor (A Great American Brand, LLC) has been owned by a partnership of its own franchisees. Recruiting in the US and Canada via awfranchising.com with freestanding and convenience-store ('C&G') formats; no public state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Convenience-store format units earn ~38% less than freestanding
riskFlags.detail: 2024 Item 19 data: 59 freestanding restaurants averaged $1,326,392 gross revenue while 53 co-located C&G (convenience & gas) restaurants averaged just $815,962 - format choice materially changes unit economics, and much of the system's footprint is non-traditional.
riskFlags.source: A&W 2025 FDD Item 19 (via SHARPSHEETS analysis)

riskFlags.title: Franchisee-owned franchisor since 2011
riskFlags.detail: Yum! Brands sold A&W in December 2011 to A Great American Brand, LLC, a partnership of A&W franchisees. Franchisee ownership aligns incentives but means the franchisor has thinner capital backing than corporate- or PE-owned peers, and most operators are single-unit owners.
riskFlags.source: A&W Franchising (franchisor site); A&W corporate history

riskFlags.title: Heavy recruitment incentives (0% royalty intro, stacked fee discounts)
riskFlags.detail: The franchisor offers a scaled royalty starting at 0% for the first three months (rising to the standard 5%), a 30% veteran discount, and multi-unit fee discounts totaling $37,500 over three units - generous terms that also signal how hard the brand is working to attract new operators after decades of US unit contraction from its 1970s peak of ~2,400 restaurants.
riskFlags.source: A&W Franchising - How Much Does an A&W Franchise Cost

liquidCapitalMin.note: Minimum liquid assets $250,000; minimum net worth $500,000.

totalInvestmentMin.note: Franchisor cites $301,095-$1,640,034 excluding real estate (low end is captive/C&G formats); SHARPSHEETS' 2025 FDD-derived freestanding range is $894,434-$1,639,906.

franchiseFee.note: 30% veteran discount ($21,000) and multi-unit discounts ($25,000/$15,000/$12,500) available.

royaltyPercent.source: A&W Franchising (franchisor site); SHARPSHEETS 2025 FDD analysis
royaltyPercent.note: Standard 5% of gross sales; scaled intro program starts at 0% for the first three months.

adFundPercent.note: 2% marketing fee per SHARPSHEETS; Vetted Biz cites combined marketing obligations of 4%.

avgUnitRevenue.note: 2024 average for 59 freestanding restaurants open the full year; C&G (convenience-store) restaurants averaged $815,962 across 53 units.

totalUnits.note: Systemwide including international (notably ~390 in Southeast Asia under a separate master); US-only directory counts vary widely (Vetted Biz lists 215 reporting US locations; brand markets ~500 US restaurants).

yearFounded.source: A&W corporate history (centennial brand, Lodi, CA root beer stand)

yearFranchising.source: A&W corporate history (pioneer of US restaurant franchising in the 1920s)
yearFranchising.note: Commonly cited as the first franchised restaurant chain in the US; Vetted Biz lists 1950 for the current franchisor entity.

trainingWeeks.source: Vetted Biz - A&W All American Food Franchise Insights
trainingWeeks.note: Approximately two weeks at the corporate training facility (Lexington, KY).

veteranDiscount.note: 30% off the franchise fee ($30,000 reduced to $21,000).

multiUnitAvailable.source: A&W Franchising (franchisor site, multi-unit fee discount schedule)

internationalAvailability.source: A&W Franchising (US and Canada opportunities; large international footprint)

fddAvailable.source: SHARPSHEETS / Franchise Direct A&W 2025 FDD summaries

item19Available.source: SHARPSHEETS A&W 2025 FDD analysis (publishes Item 19 cohorts)

brands.name: Jeremiah's Italian Ice
brands.notes: Fast-growing Orlando-based frozen dessert brand (Italian ice, soft-serve, 'gelati' layered treats) founded 1996 by Jeremy Litwack; franchising only since 2019. Celebrated its 150th open location in May 2024, with 260+ franchise units awarded across 115+ franchise groups - so a large share of the system is pipeline, not open stores. Footprint concentrated across 11 Sun Belt states (FL, AZ, GA, NC, SC, LA, CO, NV, AL, TN, TX); no published state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Very young franchise system - franchising only since 2019
riskFlags.detail: Nearly the entire franchised base opened after 2020, so long-run unit economics, renewal behavior, and failure rates are unproven; Item 19 averages rest on a small cohort of mature stores.
riskFlags.source: Jeremiah's franchise site - Our Story; Vetted Biz

riskFlags.title: Awarded pipeline (260+) far exceeds open locations (~150)
riskFlags.detail: As of May 2024 the brand had awarded over 260 franchise units against ~150 open locations - growth depends on franchisees actually building out commitments, and stacked sold-but-unopened territories can crowd future availability.
riskFlags.source: PR Newswire - Jeremiah's Italian Ice Celebrates 150th Location

riskFlags.title: Seasonal dessert category with Sun Belt concentration
riskFlags.detail: The system is concentrated in 11 southern states led by home-market Florida; frozen-dessert demand is weather-sensitive, and the franchisor also operates ~20% of units company-owned (19 of 97 at end of 2023), competing alongside franchisees in core markets.
riskFlags.source: Franchising.com Jeremiah's overview; Franchise Payback outlet data

liquidCapitalMin.source: Vetted Biz - Jeremiah's Italian Ice Franchise Insights

totalInvestmentMin.source: Jeremiah's FDD (via Vetted Biz and SHARPSHEETS)
totalInvestmentMin.note: Low end covers kiosk/non-traditional formats; standard stores run roughly $325,000-$696,000 per Franchise Chatter's FDD review.

totalInvestmentMax.source: Jeremiah's FDD (via Vetted Biz and SHARPSHEETS)

franchiseFee.source: Jeremiah's FDD (via Vetted Biz and SHARPSHEETS)
franchiseFee.note: FDD discloses $12,000-$30,000 by unit type; $30,000 is the standard store fee.

royaltyPercent.source: Jeremiah's FDD (via Vetted Biz and Franchise Chatter)

adFundPercent.source: Franchise Chatter FDD Talk review (2023 FDD)
adFundPercent.note: 2% marketing fund; Vetted Biz cites combined marketing obligations of 5% including local requirements.

avgUnitRevenue.note: Franchise Chatter's 2023 FDD review cited ~$576K average sales; estimated payback 5.9-7.9 years per Vetted Biz.

totalUnits.note: 150 open locations as of May 2024 (97 at end of 2023: 78 franchised + 19 company-owned); 60+ more openings planned within 18 months.

yearFounded.note: Founded by Jeremy Litwack in the Orlando, FL area.

yearFranchising.source: Jeremiah's franchise site - Our Story; franchise press

isGrowing.source: PR Newswire - 150th location announcement; Franchise Payback outlet data
isGrowing.note: From 97 units (end 2023) to 150 by May 2024, with 260+ units awarded.

trainingWeeks.source: Vetted Biz - Jeremiah's Italian Ice Franchise Insights
trainingWeeks.note: Two weeks at the corporate location (Orlando, FL).

seasonalityNotes.value: Frozen-dessert demand is weather-sensitive; the system mitigates this by concentrating in warm-weather Sun Belt states (FL, AZ, TX, etc.) where year-round operation is feasible.

fddAvailable.source: Vetted Biz / SHARPSHEETS / Franchise Chatter Jeremiah's FDD reviews

item19Available.source: Franchise Chatter FDD Talk review (publishes Item 19 average sales)

brands.name: Pop-A-Lock
brands.notes: America's largest mobile locksmith and roadside-assistance franchise (founded 1991 in Lafayette, LA by off-duty law enforcement officers; franchising since 1994), providing car unlocks, residential/commercial locksmithing, and security services from service vehicles across ~450 franchised territories. No company-owned units. No published open-territory list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Franchised unit count declining (~-7% over three years)
riskFlags.detail: Third-party FDD-tracking analysis counts 452 franchised US locations, a roughly 7% decrease over the past three years - a shrinking system in a category being squeezed by smart locks, connected-car remote unlock, and automaker roadside programs.
riskFlags.source: FranchiseLens Pop-A-Lock franchise analysis

riskFlags.title: Extreme franchisee performance dispersion
riskFlags.detail: FDD-derived performance analysis shows top multi-unit operators averaging $5M+ in revenue while bottom single-unit performers average around $55K - outcomes depend heavily on territory size, commercial contracts, and fleet scale.
riskFlags.source: FranchiseLens / Franchise Signal Pop-A-Lock FDD analyses

riskFlags.title: Litigation/bankruptcy disclosures present in FDD
riskFlags.detail: FDD-tracking services note Pop-A-Lock (System Forward America, Inc.) discloses lawsuits and/or bankruptcy information in its FDD Items 3/4; review the current document for specifics.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - Pop-A-Lock Franchise Insights

totalInvestmentMin.source: Pop-A-Lock 2025 FDD Item 7 (via The FDD Exchange)
totalInvestmentMin.note: $117,565.95-$190,610.95 per the 2025 FDD; Vetted Biz cites $169,565-$227,610 for larger territories.

totalInvestmentMax.source: Pop-A-Lock 2025 FDD Item 7 (via The FDD Exchange)

franchiseFee.note: Population/territory-based: $23,000-$63,000 paid to the franchisor per the 2025 FDD.

royaltyPercent.source: Pop-A-Lock FDD (via Vetted Biz and FranchiseLens)

adFundPercent.source: Pop-A-Lock FDD (via Vetted Biz and FranchiseLens)

totalUnits.note: All franchised; Vetted Biz counts 404 US locations. Declining ~7% over three years.

yearFounded.note: Founded in Lafayette, LA by law-enforcement officers.

isGrowing.note: ~-7% franchised unit change over the past three years.

mobileBased.source: Pop-A-Lock franchisor site (mobile service-vehicle model, no storefront required)

businessModelType.source: Pop-A-Lock franchisor site (consumer roadside/residential plus commercial locksmith and fleet contracts)

brands.name: Precision Tune Auto Care
brands.notes: Legacy auto repair and maintenance franchise (founded 1976 in Beaumont, TX; franchising since 1977; franchisor Precision Franchising LLC, Leesburg, VA) offering tune-ups, brakes, oil changes, and general repair. Roughly 294 centers worldwide - 232 US plus 62 international - well below the brand's historical scale. No published open-territory list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: System far below its historical peak
riskFlags.detail: Precision Tune had 100 shops by the end of the 1970s and several hundred US centers during its 1990s public-company era (Precision Auto Care, Inc.); today the US count is roughly 232. Directory counts have drifted down across recent years (Vetted Biz 236; franchisor materials cite 260+ US at earlier dates), indicating long-run contraction in a category contested by dealerships and national quick-lube chains.
riskFlags.source: Precision Tune corporate history; SEC EDGAR (Precision Auto Care, Inc. Form 10-K); Vetted Biz

riskFlags.title: Stacked ongoing fees: up to 7.5% royalty plus up to 7.5% local marketing
riskFlags.detail: Royalty runs 6%-7.5% of gross sales with a $300/week minimum regardless of revenue, plus a 1.5% national marketing fund contribution and potential local/co-op advertising obligations of up to 7.5% - combined ongoing fees can exceed 15% on some revenue.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Gator Precision Tune listing; Franchise Chatter fee analysis

riskFlags.title: Litigation/bankruptcy disclosures present in FDD
riskFlags.detail: FDD-tracking services note Precision Tune Auto Care discloses lawsuits and/or bankruptcy information in its FDD Items 3/4; the predecessor public parent also went through well-documented financial distress in the early 2000s. Review the current FDD for specifics.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - Precision Tune Auto Care Franchise Insights

liquidCapitalMin.note: $100,000 liquid; $350,000 minimum net worth. Vetted Biz cites $35,000 minimum cash.

totalInvestmentMin.note: SHARPSHEETS' 2025 FDD-derived range is $134,000-$310,000; assumes leased facility.

franchiseFee.source: Precision Tune FDD (via SHARPSHEETS and Vetted Biz)
franchiseFee.note: FDD discloses $2,000-$25,000 depending on circumstances; $25,000 standard.

royaltyPercent.source: Precision Tune FDD (via Franchise Gator and Franchise Chatter fee analysis)

adFundPercent.note: 1.5% national marketing fund plus local/co-op marketing obligations of up to 7.5%.

avgUnitRevenue.source: Vetted Biz - Precision Tune Auto Care Franchise Insights (FDD-derived)
avgUnitRevenue.note: Yearly gross sales per Vetted Biz analysis; estimated payback 3.3-5.3 years.

totalUnits.source: Franchise Gator / franchisor materials (232 US centers, 62 international, ~294 worldwide)

yearFounded.source: Precision Tune corporate site - Our History
yearFounded.note: Founded by Bill Childs in Beaumont, TX.

yearFranchising.source: Precision Tune corporate site - Our History (franchising began the year after founding)

trainingWeeks.source: Vetted Biz - Precision Tune Auto Care Franchise Insights

veteranDiscount.note: 35% off the franchise fee for veterans.

financingOffered.source: SHARPSHEETS Precision Tune FDD analysis (franchisor offers financing)

internationalAvailability.source: Precision Tune corporate site (centers in six countries)

businessModelType.source: Precision Tune franchisor site (consumer auto repair)

mobileBased.source: Precision Tune franchisor site (fixed service-center model)
